notes: Live capture now covers `inventoryAdjustQuantities`. Shopify mirrored each `available` delta into an additional `on_hand` change, returned a real `InventoryAdjustmentGroup.id`, exposed `inventoryAdjustmentGroup.app` and `changes.location.name` on the successful payload, left `quantityAfterChange` null in the mutation payload, echoed per-change `ledgerDocumentUri` values separately from the group-level `referenceDocumentUri`, updated `productVariant` / `inventoryItem.variant` quantities before product-level `totalInventory` and `inventory_total:` catalog search caught up, accepted non-available names like `incoming` only when each change carried a ledger document URI, rejected invalid names like `on_hand` with an explicit valid-values error plus the same ledger document URI requirement, and surfaced `inventoryAdjustmentGroup.staffMember` as a top-level `ACCESS_DENIED` companion error requiring `read_users` while the rest of the mutation payload still succeeded. Missing required nested change fields (`inventoryItemId`, `locationId`, `delta`) still surfaced as top-level `INVALID_VARIABLE` GraphQL errors before the mutation resolver ran.

expectedDifferences.reason: Only the numeric `productsCount(query: "inventory_total:>=14").count` is wildcarded. The live fixture ran against the full Shopify catalog, so this count can include products outside the captured first page that local replay intentionally does not hydrate. The paired `products(first: 10, query: "inventory_total:>=14")` page, each returned product's inventory fields, and the count `precision` value are still compared strictly.
